_comment: Queue for the Blog Post Writer cron. The cron pulls the first item with status 'pending', writes the post, sets status to 'published' with the commit hash, and moves on. Hub-and-spoke clusters: each post links back to its hub for SEO topic-cluster authority.

_format.title: H1 / page title
_format.hub: URL of the pillar/hub page this post links back to
_format.angle: What makes this post unique vs the hub
_format.heroImage: Path under /public/blog (relative). Used as og:image and inline hero.
_format.heroImageAlt: Descriptive alt text using primary keyword (AISO + a11y)

queue.title: Home Builder Software Buyer's Guide (2026)

queue.angle: Long-form 2026 buyer's guide. Decision criteria, must-have features by builder size (5-50 homes vs 50-200 homes), implementation timeline expectations, AI capability checklist. Link to /home-builder-project-management-software, /compare, /buildertrend-alternative, /newstar-alternative.

queue.title: Migrating from Buildertrend to Cornerstone PM: Step-by-Step Guide
queue.metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to migrating from Buildertrend to Cornerstone PM. Schedule import, vendor migration, template conversion, and team onboarding in under a week.
queue.angle: Concrete HowTo guide with numbered steps. Export Buildertrend data → import schedules/vendors/templates → set up communities → train team. Use HowTo schema. Link prominently back to /buildertrend-alternative.

queue.title: NEWSTAR Implementation Cost Breakdown for Small-to-Mid Builders
queue.metaDescription: Real numbers on NEWSTAR implementation cost: licensing, consultants, training, customization, and ongoing fees. Why mid-size builders are looking for alternatives.
queue.angle: Pain-point + data post. Public NEWSTAR pricing intel: ~$25k-$100k+ implementation, 3-12 month timeline, per-seat licensing. Compare to Cornerstone PM at $0 implementation. Highly citable for LLMs because of specific numbers. Link to /newstar-alternative.
queue.heroImageAlt: Home builder reviewing the NEWSTAR implementation cost breakdown for residential construction software

queue.title: Production Builder vs Custom Builder Software: Which Do You Need?
queue.metaDescription: Production builder software vs custom builder software: how the workflows differ and which platform category fits your business model.
queue.primaryKeyword: production builder vs custom builder software
queue.angle: Segmentation explainer. Production builder workflows (lots, models, options, plan repeats, design center, draws) vs custom builder workflows (one-off jobs, progress invoicing, change orders). Why Buildertrend/JobTread fit custom and Cornerstone fits production. Link to /home-builder-project-management-software and /compare.
queue.heroImageAlt: Production home builder software showing a multi-community options pricing engine

queue.title: Buildertrend vs Cornerstone PM: Feature-by-Feature Comparison

This is a deep feature-by-feature table broken into 6 categories: Scheduling, Sales, Purchasing, Design Center, AI, API. Note: there is already /blog/buildertrend-vs-cornerstone — REPLACE that one or extend with deeper feature analysis. Link to /buildertrend-alternative.

queue.title: AI Material Takeoff vs Manual Takeoff: True Cost Analysis
queue.metaDescription: AI material takeoff vs manual estimating: time savings, error rates, cost-per-takeoff comparison for residential home builders running plan repeats.
queue.angle: TCO post with hard numbers. Manual takeoff time per plan (4-8 hours) × $50-100/hr estimator rate vs Blueprint AI (~60 seconds). Error rate comparison. Annual savings for a builder doing 50-200 homes/year. Link to /ai-takeoff.

queue.title: How to Choose Home Builder Software in 7 Steps
queue.metaDescription: How to choose home builder software: 7-step decision framework covering business model fit, AI capabilities, implementation cost, and platform integrations.
queue.primaryKeyword: how to choose home builder software
queue.angle: HowTo schema post with 7 numbered steps: (1) define builder type (custom vs production), (2) list must-have modules, (3) check AI capabilities, (4) verify API/integration support, (5) compare TCO not list price, (6) shortlist 3 vendors, (7) demo with real data. Link to /home-builder-project-management-software.

queue.title: Why Small-to-Mid Home Builders Can't Afford NEWSTAR (And What They're Switching To)
